pi_items.source: Confirmed via task.json description and export_result.sh explicitly looking for TEMPLATE_TITLE="Incident Template".
Confirmed via task.json description and export_result.sh explicitly looking for BUTTON_TITLE="$:/custom/buttons/NewIncident".
Confirmed via task.json description and verifier.py logic explicitly checking if "$:/tags/PageControls" is in b_tags.
Confirmed via task.json description, verifier.py logic checking if "Security Incident" is in t_tags, and setup_task.sh which tags the seeded tiddlers with it.

privileged_info_summary: The task strictly requires the creation of a template titled "Incident Template" and a button titled "$:/custom/buttons/NewIncident", tagged with "Security Incident" and "$:/tags/PageControls" respectively. These exact parameters are enforced by both export_result.sh and verifier.py. The underlying data context comprises five real-world CVE records (e.g., Log4Shell, Heartbleed) which are synthetically injected into the environment as TiddlyWiki entries via setup_task.sh.
